Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Palestinian boy, 12, describes how Israeli forces killed his family in car 143 upvotes, 18 comments
  2. Jemalloc un-abandoned by Meta 79 upvotes, 17 comments
  3. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant 200 upvotes, 64 comments
  4. The “small web” is bigger than you might think 99 upvotes, 29 comments
  5. MoD sources warn Palantir role at heart of government is threat to UK security 470 upvotes, 176 comments
  6. Language Model Teams as Distrbuted Systems 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Why I love FreeBSD 224 upvotes, 87 comments
  8. Event Publisher enables event integration between Keycloak and OpenFGA 22 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. Lazycut: A simple terminal video trimmer using FFmpeg 95 upvotes, 33 comments
  10. Comparing Python Type Checkers: Typing Spec Conformance 65 upvotes, 22 comments
  11. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 558 upvotes, 277 comments
  12. Home Assistant waters my plants 200 upvotes, 92 comments
  13. The return-to-the-office trend backfires 37 upvotes, 12 comments
  14. Where does engineering go? Retreat findings and insights [pdf] 12 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Lies I was told about collaborative editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs 141 upvotes, 73 comments
  16. Speed at the cost of quality: Study of use of Cursor AI in open source projects 41 upvotes, 16 comments
  17. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 938 upvotes, 585 comments
  18. On The Need For Understanding 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  19. Even faster asin() was staring right at me 76 upvotes, 39 comments
  20. Launch HN: Voygr (YC W26) – A better maps API for agents and AI apps 34 upvotes, 16 comments
  21. Kona EV Hacking 77 upvotes, 47 comments
  22. US Job Market Visualizer 265 upvotes, 220 comments
  23. Launch HN: Chamber (YC W26) – An AI Teammate for GPU Infrastructure 8 upvotes, 2 comments
  24. Agent Skills – Open Security Database 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  25. Apideck CLI – An AI-agent interface with much lower context consumption than MCP 77 upvotes, 81 comments
  26. Cert Authorities Check for DNSSEC from Today 52 upvotes, 52 comments
  27. Starlink Mini as a failover 57 upvotes, 81 comments
  28. AirPods Max 2 85 upvotes, 161 comments
  29. The bureaucracy blocking the chance at a cure 34 upvotes, 55 comments
  30. Kaizen (YC P25) Hiring Eng, GTM, Cos to Automate BPOs 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 17 March 2026. Source here.
